causal_conclusion: The generated MQL5 runtime Atr() applied EMA smoothing (alpha=2/(n+1)) to true range instead of the frozen Wilder smoothing (alpha=1/n) mandated by the accepted layer1 ATR contract ('period-1 Wilder warmup'), the Rust reference engine (indicators::atr -> wilder), and the accepted native NoraAtr3V1 runtime. The larger EMA ATR shrinks distance_atr_abs at volatile bars; at bar 10 of trend_pullback_1 the EMA distance is 0.4997 (<=0.5, signal true) while the Wilder distance is 0.5617 (>0.5, signal false). The MQL5 runtime therefore emits an extra trend_pullback_1 trade (signal 10, entry 11, gap_stop exit 12) and an extra trend_pullback_2 trade that Rust suppresses. The defect is in MQL5 generation (lab/mql5gen/ten_strategy.py Atr); Rust, the AST, and the frozen evidence are correct.
correction: Replace the Atr() EMA smoothing with Wilder smoothing (seed SMA at i=2, recurrence (prev*2+tr)/3), matching NoraAtr3V1. EMA indicator kernel is unchanged.
mql5_trace_semantic: EMA ATR (alpha=2/(n+1)); the defective generated Atr() that matches the native CSV
